bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility for my side. The opponent’s offer is valid but gives me the lowest value on every issue, and their “fair win-win” message is not supported by the numbers, so I will counter with my strongest opening bid.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself while making only a small concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueF offer, which is very poor for me, and their “win-win” messages are not supported by the offer history.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only slightly improved their offer from all valueF to issueA:valueE while keeping the rest at valueF, which is still very low utility for me, so I treat their “win-win” language as cheap talk rather than evidence of a fair compromise. I will make a small concession only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.06), while making a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD to avoid repeating my last offer. The opponent has only slightly improved their offer for me by moving issueA from valueF to valueE while keeping the rest at valueF, so their “fair/win-win” messages are not strongly supported by the bid history.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.935 because it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers remain very poor for my preferences, with no reliable upward trend. Their messages claim fairness, but the actual bids mostly stay near valueF and even shift concessions across issues rather than improving overall for me, so I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still very early, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only made small concessions and their offers remain low-value to me, though they consistently keep issueD at valueF, so I will concede on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.89 because it is still very early (t=0.12), so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small movement on issueA/issueC, and their “fair/win-win” messages are cheap talk not matched by the numbers. I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while offering concessions on lower-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.856 because it is still early at t=0.14 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small movements. Their messages claim fairness, but the actual bids mostly keep issueB, issueC, and issueD near my worst values, so I will not make major concessions on my high-weight issues A and B yet. I am avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer by conceding slightly further on lower-weight issueC while holding issueA and issueB at my best values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.824 because it is still early at t=0.16, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved only modestly for me, mainly on issueA/issueC while keeping most other issues at valueF, so I will continue conceding first on lower-weight issues while protecting issueA and issueB.

I am still targeting a high-utility offer because the negotiation is early at t=0.18, but I am continuing concessions mainly on lower-weight issues rather than giving up my most important issueA/issueB positions. The opponent’s offers remain very low utility for me despite their “win-win” messages, with only small concessions on issueA, issueC, and issueD, so I trust the bid history more than the wording and keep pressure with a strong but somewhat softened package.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 by holding firm on my two most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.20 there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-utility proposals, though their latest offers show some movement mainly on issueA; their “win-win” messages are not enough to override the actual bid history.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 because it is early-mid negotiation at t=0.22, so I should not concede heavily on high-weight issueA and issueB yet. The opponent has meaningfully moved on issueA up to my best value but is still holding issueB/issueC/issueD mostly at valueF, so I will keep issueA and issueB favorable while making a small change on the low-weight issueD to avoid repeating my last exact bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.79, keeping my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB at valueA while using lower-weight issueC/issueD for concessions. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not move too close to the opponent’s low-utility offers; their messages claim fairness, but the actual bids remain mostly unfavorable, with only intermittent concessions on issueA, issueB, and issueD.

At t=0.26 I am still targeting a strong utility bid around 0.76, since there is no need to concede heavily this early. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for me on issueA and occasionally issueB/D, but they continue to push hard for low values on issueC and issueD, so I concede on those lower-weight issues while holding firm on my highest-weight issues A and B. Their “fair/win-win” messages are treated as cheap talk; the actual offers are still much worse for me than my counteroffers.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.805, since it is only t=0.28 and there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent’s messages claim fairness, but their actual offers remain low-value to me; their latest move gives some movement on lower-weight issueC and issueD, so I am matching concessions there while holding firm on the more important issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a utility just under 0.80, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while continuing to protect issueA and issueB, which matter most to me. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure to move from maximal demands, but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest low-utility package; their offers show some movement on issueA and issueC, while their fairness messages remain cheap talk.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, which is still strong for me but continues gradual concession at t=0.32 by giving ground mainly on lower-weight issueC and issueD while holding my high-weight issueA and issueB. The opponent’s actual bids remain low-utility for me and their “win-win” messages are not backed by much movement beyond occasional concessions, so I am not accepting their framing but am offering a new compromise rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.84, keeping firm on the high-weight issues A and B while making concessions on lower-weight C and D. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers; their actual bids show some movement on A/B but remain far from acceptable despite “fair” messaging.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.84 by holding firm on the most important issueA while making meaningful concessions on issueB, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.36 there is some time pressure to move beyond my earlier high demands, and the opponent’s actual offers show limited but real concessions especially on issueA and recently issueB, while their “fair/win-win” messages are cheap talk.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 because we are only at t=0.38, so I should concede gradually rather than jump to the opponent’s low-utility packages. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat, especially showing movement on issueA and issueB, but their natural-language claims of “fairness” overstate the actual value to me, so I am holding firm on high-weight issueA while conceding more on the lowest-weight issueD.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.78, conceding further on lower-weight issueD and moderately on issueC while holding issueA at my top value because it is most important. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat, especially on issueA and issueB, but they still mostly push issueC and issueD toward low-utility values for me; their “fair/win-win” messages are less informative than the numerical concessions. At t=0.40, a meaningful but not excessive concession is appropriate, and this bid avoids repeating my prior offer.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, conceding further from my last offer while still protecting issueA, my highest-weight issue. At t=0.42 we are in the mid-game, so I can move on lower/mid-weight issues to improve acceptability, especially since the opponent’s offers have gradually improved to issueA:valueC and issueB:valueB but still demand poor outcomes on issueC and issueD. Their “win-win” messages are cheap talk, but the actual bids suggest they may tolerate movement on issueB and issueA, so I will hold issueA while offering concessions on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.71, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting my most important issue, issueA. At t=0.44 there is some time pressure to move toward the middle, but the opponent’s latest offer is still very low for me and their “win-win” messages are not supported by the numbers. Their history suggests they may tolerate issueB at valueB and lower concessions on issueC/issueD, so I concede there first.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, which is a moderate concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting issueA, my highest-weight issue. At t=0.46 there is time to keep bargaining, but the opponent’s latest offer has improved meaningfully on issueA and issueB while holding issueC/issueD low for me, so I am conceding mostly on the lower-weight issues and not taking their “fair landing point” language at face value.

I am targeting a utility around 0.71, making a modest mid-game concession on the low-weight issueD while preserving my high-weight priorities issueA and issueB. The opponent’s actual offers suggest they strongly prefer low values on issueC and issueD and have sometimes moved on issueA/issueB, while their “fair/win-win” messages are cheap talk rather than binding evidence.
